question: Compare the NuScale RPV dimensions from Table 5.3-1 with the visual representation in Figure 5.3-1.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures in place to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to prevent exceeding pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits under anticipated operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV is a key component of the reactor coolant system, serving as a barrier to fission product release and providing structural support for various reactor systems. Materials used for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant's design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the information for regulatory purposes, and any copies must include the appropriate copyright and proprietary notices.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fastener requirements for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies procedures for maintaining a dry, controlled environment during shipment and lists the key codes and standards used for quality assurance and construction.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel Materials section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, providing a crucial barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across various power levels by establishing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures, using both software and manual calculations. It accounts for uncertainties in flow resistance and thermal head, ensuring efficient operation, safety margins, and reliable natural circulation with minimal pressure losses in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

edges.description: Section B provides the general description and context for the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section A discusses the operational procedures and requirements to ensure the RPV's integrity, with both sections connected by their focus on the RPV's function and regulatory compliance.

Section B provides the copyright and usage preface for the NuScale Design Certification Application, which includes the technical content such as the RPV dimensions referenced in Section A's discussion of operating procedures.

Section A provides specific technical details about the NuScale Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which are elaborated upon and visually represented in the figures and tables referenced in the broader context introduced by Section B's preface.

Section A discusses the shipment, handling, and storage requirements for the RPV, while Section B addresses the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded; both sections are related through their focus on maintaining the integrity and safety of the reactor vessel under different conditions.

Section A discusses inspection, shipment, and storage requirements for the RPV, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV's function and configuration, with Section A referencing the component described in Section B.

Section A provides specific inspection, shipment, and handling requirements for the NuScale RPV, while Section B is a general preface to the chapter containing the RPV dimensions and figures, thus Section A specifies details for the general context introduced in Section B.

Section A provides the physical and structural description of the NuScale RPV, while Section B discusses the operational procedures developed to ensure the RPV's pressure-temperature limits (which are determined by its dimensions and materials) are not exceeded, thus connecting the vessel's design characteristics to its safe operation.

Section A discusses the system evaluation and performance criteria for the NuScale Power Plant, while Section B addresses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity, both of which rely on accurate knowledge of RPV dimensions as provided in Table 5.3-1 and depicted in Figure 5.3-1.

Section A references the system evaluation and performance criteria, while Section B provides the specific description and context for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) whose dimensions are compared in Table 5.3-1 and visually represented in Figure 5.3-1.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) as part of the reactor coolant system, while Section B discusses the need for operating procedures to ensure the RPV's pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, connecting the design basis to operational compliance.

Section B provides a detailed description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which is a component introduced in the general system overview in Section A, thereby specifying the details of a subsystem mentioned in Section A.
Section A discusses the evaluation of system performance parameters, which depend on the RPV dimensions specified in Section B's design basis, connecting the two through the use of RPV design values.
